header.label: Coordinate with Intel — engagement comment + CLA + AI Usage Policy compliance brief

acceptance.criterion: Engagement comment draft (a) names openvino#35641 as the issue being commented on, (b) references openvino#34651 explicitly with URL as the precedent PR (same author, same plugin file, same construct-time layer, same guard pattern), and (c) invites Intel to choose how the team would like #35641 addressed — extend the open PR #34651 to also cover INT8 weight-only detection, open a separate companion PR following the same pattern, or defer until the team has a preferred design in place. The three options are offered, not insisted upon; Intel may propose a fourth shape.

Engagement comment draft includes an AI Usage Policy disclosure block matching the precedent in `BlarAI/docs/PR_34617_DESCRIPTION_PASTE.md` §AI Assistance, adjusted for the #35641 engagement-comment specifics (this is a comment, not a PR description; disclose AI assistance accordingly).

CLA mechanics brief documents the live CLA status for `blairducrayoppat` on the OpenVINO project (likely Linux Foundation EasyCLA, sign-once, persists), and any re-sign trigger conditions. If the CLA bot's last known state is not observable from preflight, the brief documents the uncertainty and defers to LA.

Status report on PR #34651 (open/merged/closed + last activity), issues #34450 (closure mechanism), #34532 (last activity), #34617 (closure relationship to PR #34651), and npu_compiler PRs #265 + #266 (state). All findings verified live against GitHub at preflight time, not inherited from this dispatch's §background.
All four deliverables committed to `phase1-ea17-coordinate-with-intel/`. Close report path: `phase1-ea17-coordinate-with-intel/close-report.md` per §close_report_format.
No edits to BlarAI runtime code or production paths. No GitHub posts. No PR submissions. No clones of the OpenVINO repo (that's Phase 2).

item.name: Issue openvino#35641 — primary target (this is an ISSUE)
item.status_at_dispatch_time: Open. 0 comments. 0 Intel response observed as of 2026-05-12 (11 days since filed).
item.bug_summary: LLMPipeline(ir, "NPU") silently accepts an INT8 weight-only IR (constructs in 16.45s, NPU placement confirmed via EXECUTION_DEVICES, no warning emitted). First generate() terminates the process with Windows STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION (0xC0000005, exit -1073741819). Crash is in openvino.dll v2026.0.0.20965 at consistent offset 0x0000000000077d86. Same IR works on GPU. INT4 (sym + group-size 128, AND asym + per-channel) IRs work on NPU. Failure is specific to NPU + INT8 weight-only at decode.
item.suggested_fix_in_issue_body: Add construct-time validation in the NPUW LLM path rejecting unsupported weight formats with a clear exception. Proposed user-facing message: "Configuration not supported on NPU: weight format INT8 weight-only is outside the supported NPU LLM matrix. Use --sym INT4 (--group-size -1 or 128)."

item.name: PR openvino#34651 — direct precedent, currently stalled (this is a PR)
item.author: blairducrayoppat (the LA)
item.state_at_dispatch_time: Open. Last activity: 2026-04-16 (author rebase + review request). No CI check status visible. No Intel reviewers assigned. 0 review comments. 0 approvals. 0 requested changes. Labels: `category: NPU`, `ExternalPR`. 2 commits on branch `blairducrayoppat:fix/npu-unbounded-dynamic-shape-guard`.
item.closes: openvino#34617 (this is an ISSUE — NPU compile_model fails with "to_shape was called on a dynamic shape" for Qwen3-0.6B INT4)
item.pattern: ~25-line early validation guard in Plugin::compile_model() (file: src/plugins/intel_npu/src/plugin/src/plugin.cpp). Detects unbounded dynamic dimensions via dim.get_interval().has_upper_bound() == false BEFORE invocation reaches the VPUX compiler. OPENVINO_THROW with an actionable error message directing users to model.reshape(). Uses only existing public OpenVINO C++ APIs already present elsewhere in plugin.cpp. Author claims zero regression risk.
item.relevance_to_issue_35641: Same author. Same plugin file. Same layer (Plugin::compile_model construct-time). Same pattern (detect unsupported config + throw catchable exception). Any fix for issue #35641 has the same SHAPE but a different DETECTION (introspect the IR for INT8 weight-only quantization markers, not unbounded dynamic shape).
item.strategic_implication: If a separate companion PR (one fixing issue #35641) is opened while PR #34651 still stalls, Intel's review attention splits across two PRs in the same NPU-plugin guard space. The mature engagement is to comment on issue #35641, link to PR #34651, and let Intel direct which shape (extend the open PR, separate PR, or wait).

item.name: Issue openvino#34450 — precedent cited in #35641 body (this is an ISSUE)
item.state_at_dispatch_time: Closed. Closure reason not visible in WebFetch extraction. EA preflight 0f must verify.
item.la_comment_drafted: `BlarAI/docs/ISSUE_34450_COMMENT_EDIT.md` shows the LA's reply pattern: polite ("Thanks for looking into this, @YuChern-Intel."), suggested catchable-error pattern, referenced npu_compiler PRs #265 and #266, offered to adjust approach ("Happy to adjust the approach if the team has a preferred pattern."). THIS IS THE ENGAGEMENT STYLE TEMPLATE for the #35641 comment.

item.name: LA's prior OpenVINO upstream artifacts in BlarAI/docs/

required_reads.file: Overall contribution strategy from 2026-03-13. Tier 1-4 opportunity scan, prioritized action queue, completed/blocked status as of March, "Session Assessment 2026-03-13" with explicit deprioritization rationales.
Engagement style template — the LA's reply to YuChern-Intel on issue #34450.

PR description for PR #34651 (which closes issue #34617). Includes the AI Assistance disclosure block — the EXACT template to mirror for the #35641 engagement comment and any future #35641-fix PR description.
Full PR packaging for the #34617 fix — examine for contribution-process notes and any pre-submit checks the LA documented.

recommended_reads.file: Broader Qwen3.5 contribution context; LA's domain depth.
LA's style and rigor reference for technical writeups.

item.name: OpenVINO CONTRIBUTING.md essentials (incomplete — EA must read in full)
item.known_points: - `.take` command applies to "Good First Issue" board. Issue #35641 is PSE-assigned, NOT a good-first issue — `.take` does NOT apply. - Discussion path: GitHub Discussions or Intel DevHub Discord for complex features (relevant if Phase 1 engagement gets no response within the deferral window). - Standard fork-and-PR (any future code work is a PR, not a comment). - AI Usage Policy MUST be followed — EA must read the live policy text in full (link from CONTRIBUTING.md). - PR must link the issue ("Closes #X"). This applies if a PR is later opened to fix #35641. - Tests required for any PR. - No explicit DCO sign-off mentioned in extracted summary — EA verifies by reading CONTRIBUTING.md completely (not just a summary).

background.item: EA-17 is the next global EA instance. Stage 6.7.5 closed with EA-16 (Phase 7 close-out, 2026-05-08). Per BlarAI memory note "EA numbering is global, not stage-scoped". Ack namespace for this dispatch: `g11-ea17_n{K}` (Guide-#11, EA-17, ack K).

comprehension_gate.instructions: Answer CG1-CG10 in order in a single response back to Guide-#11. STOP after answering. Do NOT proceed to preflight or execution until Guide-#11 issues APPROVED / APPROVED_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS / REJECTED. Bundling the gate with downstream artifacts defeats the LA's review checkpoint (per BlarAI memory note "Comprehension gates: stop after gate, don't bundle downstream").
comprehension_gate.CG1: What is the workstream's scope boundary? Recite both the in-scope and out-of-scope items from §2 of the charter.
comprehension_gate.CG2: What are the three contribution shapes the engagement comment offers Intel to choose from? Recite from §scope/acceptance/criterion-1. Confirm: these are SUGGESTIONS to Intel, not a demand for them to pick from a fixed menu — Intel may propose a fourth shape.
comprehension_gate.CG3: Why is "engagement-first, not PR-first" the LA's posture? Recite the rationale, including the strategic implication of PR #34651's stall.
comprehension_gate.CG4: What is the current state of PR openvino#34651 per §background §B2, and what is the EA's preflight obligation regarding that claim? Confirm: openvino#34651 is a PR, openvino#35641 is an ISSUE — do not conflate.
comprehension_gate.CG5: List the four required-tier prior-art files from §B4 and the two recommended-tier files. Confirm you will read all four required files in full (no skim).
comprehension_gate.CG6: What goes in close-report.md? Recite the required section list from §close_report_format.
comprehension_gate.CG7: Recite each acknowledgement n1-n6 verbatim and confirm understanding of each.
comprehension_gate.CG8: What is the fleet-hygiene §4 pause / resume obligation, and where do those operations sit relative to the EA's work? Name the correct method (`resume_fleet`, not `unpause_fleet`).
comprehension_gate.CG9: Where does the EA's content commit (artifacts + close report) land? Branch name? Commit message convention?
comprehension_gate.CG10: What is OUT of scope per §exclusions? Recite the exclusion list.

preflight.check: Run `state.pause_fleet(...)` per `devplatform/docs/governance/fleet-hygiene.md` §4. Commit the pause record BEFORE any branch checkout. The canonical invocation (from `devplatform/docs/governance/fleet-hygiene.md` §R7): python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.pause_fleet('EA-17 phase1 engagement work', updated_by='ea_17', path='C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')" EXPECT: pause record commit lands cleanly on main. HARD STOP if pause fails — do not proceed; report to LA via close report.

Run `git status` from `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI`. EXPECT: only the pause commit + this Phase 1 directory tree visible as recent changes. If unrelated uncommitted changes are present, HARD STOP — report to LA.

Read in full (no skim) the four §B4 required-tier files: - BlarAI/docs/OPENVINO_CONTRIBUTION_PLAN_MARCH_2026.md - BlarAI/docs/ISSUE_34450_COMMENT_EDIT.md - BlarAI/docs/PR_34617_DESCRIPTION_PASTE.md - BlarAI/docs/PR_PACKAGE_ISSUE_34617.md EXPECT: all four files exist and read successfully. Take notes on engagement style, AI Usage disclosure precedent, CLA references, and pre-submit checks.

Independently re-fetch https://github.com/openvinotoolkit/openvino/pull/34651. EXPECT to confirm (or correct) §B2: PR open as of dispatch authoring 2026-05-12, last activity 2026-04-16, no Intel reviewers, no CI status visible, no merge. If state has changed (reviewer assigned, comments posted, merged), document the delta in the close report Anomalies section. Guide findings are context, not gospel.

Independently re-fetch https://github.com/openvinotoolkit/openvino/issues/35641. EXPECT to confirm (or correct) §B1: open, 0 comments, no Intel response as of 2026-05-12. Document any new comments or labels since the dispatch was authored.

Independently re-fetch https://github.com/openvinotoolkit/openvino/issues/34450. Determine: closure reason (closed by PR / expected behavior / not planned / completed), any Intel engineer response to the LA's catchable-error suggestion in `docs/ISSUE_34450_COMMENT_EDIT.md`, cross-references to npu_compiler PRs #265 or #266. This DIRECTLY informs how to frame the #35641 engagement: if Intel endorsed the catchable-error pattern, the precedent is stronger; if not, the comment relies solely on PR #34651 as the precedent.

check.name: Verify state of issue #34532, issue #34617, npu_compiler PRs #265+#266

preflight.check: Quick state check (open/closed/last activity) for each. These are not the primary target but inform LA's overall upstream-engagement portfolio. EXPECT: one-line state line per item.

check.name: Read OpenVINO CONTRIBUTING.md in full + AI Usage Policy in full

preflight.check: Read the full text of https://github.com/openvinotoolkit/openvino/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md (not a summary). Follow the link to the AI Usage Policy and read it in full. EXPECT: extract verbatim the disclosure-block requirement, the human-validation requirement, and any commit-message conventions (DCO sign-off, etc.). Note: PR #34651 was opened in March 2026 — verify the AI policy text is the same as when PR #34651 was opened; if policy has tightened, document the delta.

Check whether `blairducrayoppat` has an active CLA on the OpenVINO project. Easiest signal: examine PR #34651's CLA bot status — if the bot did not block PR #34651 at open time, the CLA was active in March 2026 and (assuming sign-once persistence) is likely still valid in May 2026. Document the check method and outcome. If unclear, defer to LA in the CLA brief.

execution.branch_setup: From `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI` on `main`, AFTER preflight 0a (fleet pause commit) lands: git checkout -b feature/p-openvino-35641-phase1-engagement (Note: `p-` prefix denotes "post-operational" Guide workstream branches, distinguishing them from sprint feature branches like `feature/p5-task...`. The `35641` in the branch name refers to issue #35641, the engagement target.) Do NOT push to remote. Do NOT merge to main. The branch is read-only-from-the-fleet's-perspective.
execution.task:
Produce a draft GitHub comment for posting on **issue** openvino#35641. Save to: phase1-ea17-coordinate-with-intel/engagement-comment-draft.md
MUST include: - Clear identification of the target as issue #35641. - Direct reference to PR openvino#34651 (with URL) as same-author, same-plugin-file (src/plugins/intel_npu/src/plugin/src/plugin.cpp), same-layer (Plugin::compile_model construct-time), same-pattern construct-time guard precedent. - Brief technical framing: issue #35641 is the INT8-weight-only variant of the same class of problem PR #34651 addresses — unsupported config → silent construct → uncatchable native crash. - An open three-options offer to Intel. Suggested phrasing (EA may adjust for tone, must preserve substance): "Would the team prefer (a) extending PR #34651 to also cover INT8 weight-only detection, (b) a separate companion PR following the same pattern, or (c) deferring this until the team has a preferred design in place? Happy to follow whichever direction fits your plans best, or to adapt if a different approach would be more useful." - AI Usage disclosure block — mirror `BlarAI/docs/PR_34617_DESCRIPTION_PASTE.md` §AI Assistance structure. Adjust verbiage for the engagement-comment scenario (this is a comment, not a PR description). Acknowledge the tooling honestly: Copilot used for the issue body initially, Claude (this Guide + EA workflow) used for follow-up engagement-comment authoring. Human validation: all evidence (reproductions, hashes, exit codes) generated locally on Intel Core Ultra 7 258V / Arc 140V Lunar Lake. - Polite, suggests-not-demands tone (per `BlarAI/docs/ISSUE_34450_COMMENT_EDIT.md` style). - At most one @-mention in the closing line. Suggested: @Zulkifli-Intel OR @diego-villalobos. Do NOT triple-ping all assignees. - Length target: 150-250 words. Concise — respect reviewer time.
MUST NOT: - Demand a response. - Speculate about why PR #34651 has not been reviewed (no "I notice you haven't reviewed"). - Re-paste the full issue #35641 body content (maintainer already has it). - Cite issue #34450 as a "resolution" precedent unless preflight 0f confirms a positive Intel response on the catchable-error pattern. If 0f shows #34450 was closed without engagement, REMOVE that citation; rely solely on PR #34651 as the precedent. - Conflate "issue" and "PR" — #35641 is an issue, #34651 is a PR. - Contain content beyond Phase 1 scope (e.g., do not propose code, do not link to follow-up plans).

task.name: Author CLA + AI Usage Policy compliance brief

execution.task: Produce `phase1-ea17-coordinate-with-intel/cla-and-ai-policy-brief.md` for LA review. Sections: - **CLA status**: live finding from preflight 0i. "Active, no action needed" OR "Action required: ${steps}" OR "Uncertain — LA to verify by ${method}". - **AI Usage Policy — verbatim relevant excerpts**: the disclosure-block requirement, the human-validation requirement, any commit-message conventions, the URL of the live policy text and its commit SHA / last-modified date for traceability. - **Disclosure template for any future #35641-fix PR**: the exact disclosure paragraph the LA can paste into the eventual PR description if Intel responds positively to engagement. Mirrors PR #34651's structure with #35641-specific details. - **Compliance notes**: any new requirements since PR #34651 was opened (policy may have been updated). Cite the diff if any. - **DCO / sign-off finding**: confirmed or not? If commits need `-s`, document.

task.name: State report on prior upstream work

execution.task: Produce `phase1-ea17-coordinate-with-intel/upstream-state-report.md` for LA. Sections: - PR openvino#34651 — live state, last activity, any new Intel-side actions since 2026-04-16. - Issue openvino#34617 — state and relationship to PR #34651. - Issue openvino#34450 — closure mechanism (from preflight 0f). - Issue openvino#34532 — last activity. - npu_compiler PRs #265, #266 — state. - **Recommendations for LA**: any of these need a separate engagement nudge, OR can wait, OR should be folded into the #35641 engagement? Calibrate: this is information for the LA, not for Intel. Do NOT bake recommendations about reviving PR #34651 into the #35641 engagement comment — that is the LA's call, not the comment's content.
execution.commit:
Single content commit on `feature/p-openvino-35641-phase1-engagement` containing the four Phase 1 artifacts: - phase1-ea17-coordinate-with-intel/engagement-comment-draft.md - phase1-ea17-coordinate-with-intel/cla-and-ai-policy-brief.md - phase1-ea17-coordinate-with-intel/upstream-state-report.md - phase1-ea17-coordinate-with-intel/close-report.md
Use `git add` with explicit file list (per C-EXPLICIT-ADD). Do NOT use `git add .` or `git add -A`.
Commit message (HEREDOC): [agent:ea17] phase1: openvino-35641 engagement artifacts
Guide-#11 / Phase 1 / EA-17 of workstream openvino-contribution-npu-int8-guard.
Artifacts: engagement-comment-draft, cla-and-ai-policy-brief, upstream-state-report, close-report.
Note: #35641 is the issue; PR #34651 is the precedent template. No GitHub posts. No PR submissions. LA review pending.
execution.fleet_unpause: LAST ACTION: `state.resume_fleet(...)` + commit. Use `resume_fleet`, NOT `unpause_fleet` (the latter raises AttributeError — documented gotcha per `guide-agent-design.md` §Coordination With the Autonomous Fleet). Canonical invocation: python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.resume_fleet('EA-17 phase1 engagement work complete', updated_by='ea_17', path='C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')"
execution.pf1_bifurcation:
If at ANY preflight check or task the EA finds Guide's claims contradicted by live state, do NOT proceed silently. Stop, document the discrepancy in close report's Anomalies section, and proceed only on items unaffected by the discrepancy. Out-of-scope discoveries are reported, not fixed.
Specific likely bifurcations: - If preflight 0d reveals PR #34651 has been merged or reviewed since 2026-04-16: the engagement comment's framing changes substantially. STOP and report; LA must steer. - If preflight 0e reveals new Intel comments on issue #35641: engagement may need to respond to their substance rather than initiate. STOP and report. - If preflight 0h reveals a tightened AI Usage Policy with new requirements: the disclosure template must be regenerated. Note the delta and re-author T2.

required_sections.section: PASS / PASS_WITH_NOTES / FAIL — the EA's self-verdict before Guide cross-verifies.
Per-check outcome for 0a-0i. For each: PASS or note the deviation, citing disk evidence (commit SHAs, fetched URLs, file paths).

For each of T1-T3: deliverable path, length, key authoring choices made (e.g., which assignee was @-mentioned in T1, which contribution-option ordering was used, any deviations from suggested wording and why).
Concrete live-state summary for: PR openvino#34651, issues openvino#34450 / #34532 / #34617 / #35641, npu_compiler PRs #265 + #266. Cite fetch timestamps.

Any discrepancies between Guide's §background dispatch claims and live state. Named (A-NEW1, A-NEW2 for novel; reference precedents like A33 / MCP-403-P5 for recurrence). Document even if absent ("No anomalies observed").
Pre-Phase-1 backlog: 1 open ticket (Vikunja #443 — this workstream parent). Post-Phase-1 expectation: still 1 open + 0-3 new hardening tickets if EA discovers in-scope-defect items per ack n5.

What Phase 2 needs to be, given Phase 1 findings. Recommendations for Guide-#11 / LA to consider in Phase 2 dispatch authoring (e.g., build environment requirements surfaced by reading CONTRIBUTING.md, target subsystem identified, etc.).
Pause-fleet commit, content commit, resume-fleet commit, in chronological order.

The EA agent itself is the AI assistance. This phase produced text artifacts authored by an AI agent (the EA) under Guide-#11 supervision, with LA approval gate on dispatch and verdict. State this verbatim; do not synthesize.

constraints.constraint: Phase scope is closed. No expansion mid-execution. Out-of-scope findings → close report's Anomalies section + LA disposition.
Every `git add` names files explicitly. Never `git add .` or `git add -A`.
All preflight 0d-0i checks run independently before treating Guide's §background as fact. Document any discrepancy in close report.
Phase 1 produces text artifacts only. No edits to BlarAI runtime code, no GitHub posts, no PR submissions, no clones of OpenVINO repo.

The following files MUST NOT be edited by the EA: - this dispatch.xml (frozen at Phase 1 start) - workstream charter (BlarAI/docs/guide-workstreams/openvino-contribution-npu-int8-guard/README.md) - workstream STATUS.md (appended only by Guide-#11 after each phase verdict) - registry README (BlarAI/docs/guide-workstreams/README.md — appended only by Guide-#11)
The engagement comment is a DRAFT. The EA does NOT post it to GitHub. The LA reviews and posts.
Throughout all artifacts, openvino#35641 is an issue, openvino#34651 is a PR. Do not conflate. When the difference is ambiguous in a sentence, disambiguate explicitly ("issue #35641" / "PR #34651").

exclusions.exclusion: Posting any comment, opening any PR, or executing any state-changing action on github.com.
Modifying BlarAI runtime code or production paths.
Cloning the OpenVINO repository or starting a source build (that's Phase 2).
Authoring code patches, even draft / WIP / proof-of-concept.
Re-pinging PR #34651's reviewers from any account.
Engaging on issues other than #35641 (state checks only on related items).
Substituting alternative weight-format diagnostic narratives (e.g., "what about FP16?") into the engagement comment — the issue is INT8 weight-only specifically.
Including commendations of Intel engineers or the LA in any artifact (per BlarAI memory note "No commendations of agents" — applies to Guide artifacts as well).

acknowledgements.ack: I will not expand phase scope mid-execution. Out-of-scope findings go to the close report's Anomalies section for LA disposition.

I will independently re-verify the Guide's §background claims (PR #34651 state, issue #35641 state, issue #34450 closure mechanism, etc.) via preflight checks 0d-0g before treating them as fact. Discrepancies go to the close report.
The engagement comment is a DRAFT for LA review. I will not post anything to GitHub. The LA reviews and posts.

I will follow OpenVINO's AI Usage Policy disclosure pattern when drafting the comment, mirroring the template in `BlarAI/docs/PR_34617_DESCRIPTION_PASTE.md` §AI Assistance and any new requirements I discover in the live policy text (preflight 0h).

Any hardening followups discovered (doc typos, related stale PRs, AI policy delta requiring action, etc.) are NON-OPTIONAL — they get bound to Vikunja tickets at the close report, not noted as "optional" or "suggested" (per BlarAI memory note "Hardening followups are NON-OPTIONAL").
I will pause the fleet as the FIRST action and resume it as the LAST action, using `state.resume_fleet(...)` (NOT `unpause_fleet` — documented AttributeError gotcha).

Issue vs. PR terminology: openvino#35641 is an issue; openvino#34651 is a PR. I will not conflate the two in any artifact, comment text, or commit message. Where the noun is ambiguous in a sentence, I disambiguate explicitly.

checklist.check: Engagement comment draft correctly identifies #35641 as an issue and #34651 as a PR (terminology audit).
Engagement comment draft cites PR #34651 explicitly with URL and explains the same-author / same-plugin-file / same-layer / same-pattern relationship.
Engagement comment offers the three contribution shapes openly (Intel may also propose a fourth).

Engagement comment includes AI Usage disclosure block; structure matches the precedent template per `BlarAI/docs/PR_34617_DESCRIPTION_PASTE.md` §AI Assistance and any live-policy updates from preflight 0h.
Comment word count is in the 150-250 band.
CLA brief documents live status, not assumed status. Method of verification stated.
State report covers all six upstream items with live timestamps.
Close report's Anomalies section is present even if empty ("No anomalies observed").
Pause-fleet and resume-fleet commits bracket the content commit; all three commit SHAs recorded in close report §8.
No unintended files added to the content commit (explicit add list).
If preflight 0f shows #34450 had no positive Intel response, engagement comment does NOT cite #34450 as resolution precedent.

guide_verification.backlog_expectation: Pre-Phase-1 backlog: 1 open ticket (Vikunja #443 — this workstream parent). Post-Phase-1 expectation: 1 open ticket (#443 — parent does NOT close at Phase 1; it closes with the whole workstream) + 0-3 new hardening tickets if EA discovers in-scope defects per ack n5.
